meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solve Deployment Issues with Antigravity
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment bottlenecks. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures stem from environment drift and configuration bloat. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your infrastructure-as-code, ensuring parity between local and production environments. Pairing this with Claude Code automates your CI/CD pipeline, allowing you to ship production-ready, scalable applications for your clients in record time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client project, the design is pixel-perfect, but the deployment server is throwing an cryptic 502 error that feels like a brick wall. You're a freelancer, not a full-time DevOps engineer; you need to ship, not debug infrastructure for three days.

This is the silent killer of freelance profitability: deployment friction. When you spend more time configuring Docker containers and environment variables than writing features, your margins evaporate. Enter the Antigravity project template—a battle-tested, opinionated framework designed to bypass the 'it works on my machine' trap and get your code into the wild consistently.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Start by cloning the baseline template to ensure all security headers and environment schemas are pre-configured for production readiness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard SaaS scaffold with Postgres, Redis, and Vercel edge runtime configurations.

steps.title: Configure Environment Parity
steps.description: Sync your local environment variables with the production schema to prevent runtime crashes during the initial deployment attempt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my .env.example file and generate a schema validation script that enforces required environment variables before deployment.

steps.title: Automate Infrastructure Provisioning
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the necessary Terraform or Pulumi files to manage your cloud resources via Antigravity's infrastructure patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular infrastructure definition file for a managed database and serverless function endpoint using Antigravity best practices.

steps.title: Implement Automated Health Checks
steps.description: Integrate automated smoke tests that run post-deployment to verify that critical routes are functional before the client sees the app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of health-check tests that verify API connectivity and database read/write access for the production environment.

steps.title: Execute Production Deployment
steps.description: Run the final deployment sequence, leveraging the template's built-in rollback logic to ensure zero downtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Execute the production build and deployment sequence, ensuring all logs are piped to the Antigravity dashboard for monitoring.

pro_tips: Always use a 'pre-flight' check script that validates your database connection string before the build process begins.
Tag your production builds with git SHAs to enable instant rollbacks if a regression hits the client site.
Keep your infrastructure code in a separate folder from your application logic to simplify future migrations.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your client's specific cloud provider preferences for faster scaffolding next time.
Implement automated database migrations within your deployment pipeline to avoid manual schema updates.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, implement a monitoring dashboard to track error rates, then move on to adding automated integration tests.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your stack and start delivering value that keeps clients coming back. By adopting a standardized deployment workflow, you transform from a struggling developer into a high-leverage architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
